title: CIP-309 chunked byte-string array
description: A CBOR `[1* bstr .size (1..64)]`: a non-empty array of byte-string chunks, each 1..64 bytes, that concatenate to a single logical byte string. Used to carry oversized byte strings (COSE_Sign1, COSE_Key, hybrid KEM ciphertext) within CBOR-map-value chunk limits. Each chunk is a CBOR `bstr`; since JSON has no byte-string type, each chunk is modelled as a lowercase-hex JSON string of 1..64 bytes (2..128 hex characters, even length). A zero-length chunk is rejected with the same CHUNK_TOO_LARGE code as an oversized chunk. STRUCTURAL only: the reassembled total length (e.g. exactly 1120 bytes for an mlkem768x25519 kem_ct) is a domain-pass check, not expressible here.
